title: Tapping for Turning 40
subtitle: An EFT sequence for the complicated feelings around reaching a milestone birthday
excerpt: An EFT tapping practice for the feelings that arrive around turning 40: the grief for what didn't happen, the comparisons with where you thought you'd be, the cultural noise about what 40 means. Works through the weight of the number toward a clearer relationship with the decade.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use in the weeks around a significant birthday, or any time the number of your age is carrying weight it shouldn't.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for the feelings around turning 40. The script works through the grief of the unlived life, the comparison pressure, and the cultural stories about what 40 means, toward what is available right now.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though turning 40 feels bigger and heavier than I expected, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I am grieving some things I thought I would have done or been by now, I accept where I actually am.
Even though the culture has a lot to say about what 40 means and most of it is not kind, I am open to deciding for myself.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: The weight of the number 40.
Side of Eye: The things I assumed I'd have done by now.
Under Eye: Comparing my 40 to where others seem to be.
Under Nose: The cultural story that says 40 is something to recover from.
Chin: The grief of the younger version of me who had a different plan.
Collarbone: The panic that says there isn't enough time left.
Under Arm: The confusion between 40 meaning something and 40 meaning nothing.
Top of Head: Forty years of a life that happened.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: 40 is a number. I decide what it means.
Side of Eye: I have forty years of things I know and understand. That is worth something.
Under Eye: The years ahead are not diminished by the years behind.
Under Nose: I can grieve the unlived life and still claim this one.
Chin: What I know now that I didn't know at 30 is real.
Collarbone: There is still time. There is plenty here.
Under Arm: I am forty years old and I am here. That is enough.
Top of Head: This decade is mine to shape. I am starting now.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
